review_text: Winner of 2000 Academy Awards for Best Picture, Best Actor (Kevin Spacey) and Best Director (Sam Mendes).

I don't believe this to be a masterpiece, nor what it is chalked up to be. More or less than that to be frank. This is a very subjective film, but what is not subjective is the morbidness of Lester's lust for Ashley, Jane's friend. I actually did not care for that part of this film. I found it disinteresting and repetitive. However, I loved Jane and Ricky's portion. It was beautiful, and I would have loved this movie more than I hate it if it was just that put into center play. This movie could have been amazing without the odd age-attraction and I'm not sure why they included it to begin with. The irony of the movie being called "American Beauty" but the entirety of the execution is an older, burnt out man wanting to have sex with a teenager until the ending, where he does redeem himself. This could have been executed entirely better, but It is the same monotonous trash Lolita tried to spew and call art. It feels beautifully horrid.
There is no subtlety or nuance to this movie. All of the themes and ideas just slap you in the face, and they aren't really profound in 2026. It does hold your attention, though.

This movie has some unexpectedly useful themes to it. I'm going to pick out one in particular that won't be popular with the internet puritan crowd. If you find yourself in a very unethical situation with a young woman that you shouldn't be with, you CAN stop before it's too late. It doesn't make you a hero, just a chance at redemption to turn away from scumbaggery to decency and humanity in the end. I know there's an ick factor...it's not supposed to make you comfortable and think everything is just a-okay, but it does mean that there are shades of gray to human behavior and all of its messy nuances. Now proceed to bash the actor portraying the message, all of the bad things he and others did in the interim (in the film and real life), and everything else one can find to bash in this dark comedy. Take me on for pointing it out while you are at it.
As good as I remembered.
Just perfect. "But don’t worry. You will someday" :)
Sad society thinking this is a masterpiece, acting is good the rest is garbage, it’s no wonder our world is corrupt!
One of my top 10 movies of all time -- as good today as it was when I saw it in the theater 25+ years ago.

This used to be a great film, but it hasn't aged well. It is now seen as offensive and inappropriate. "American Beauty" is still a good film. But with the Epstein thing hanging over us, a film like this would be severely scrutinized.

A film that touches on many topics that are controversial or close to home for some. American Beauty is depressing, dark, yet beautiful and revealing. Every character's identity crisis is played amazingly well, and the light it shines on suburban lifestyle is accurate and underrated.
I think about this film all the time. A must-watch.
I mean, like the audience score suggests, I liked the movie as well. I also understand why it's so controversial in todays times. It's a good one to watch.

Film is an engrossing observation of a regular man becoming free. Slowly, several stories of the family members combine into the last night of Lester’s life, which all seem equally fitting and meaningful at their end. Some of the effects are outdated, but are only used as artistic flourishes. Annette Bening and Steve Lacy really knock it out of the park with their roles. The girls do okay. Scenes are equally powerful being funny, uncomfortable, suspenseful, and sexy… except for the fact that it’s a couple teenage girls.

American Beauty is a cinematic masterstroke—witty, devastating, and profoundly insightful. Sam Mendes and Alan Ball dissect suburban malaise, desire, and the illusions we live by with surgical precision, while Kevin Spacey and Annette Bening deliver performances of rare depth. Fifty minutes in, every frame feels charged with meaning; two hours later, you’re left both unsettled and awed. Simply put, it’s one of the greatest films ever made—timeless, intelligent, and unflinchingly honest.
I literally regret watching this. All the characters are psychotic. And Lester was a perverted Jerk face, it’s a very depressing movie. Don’t recommend it. I prefer The Matrix.
It's hard to call this underrated when it won Best Picture, but I think it kind of is. What a masterpiece.

I know this movie was and is "a thing" in the cultural zeitgeist, but seeing it for the first time 25 years after it came out, it just didn't hit like that for me. The kids speak unlike any teenager I have ever heard. It is like an adult man wrote the script and has completely forgotten or never experienced what friends at that age act like. Maybe it was a victim of my high expectations because of how critics talk about this movie, but I thought it was very, very good, but not an all-timer. It is very original. I cannot say I have ever watched another movie quite like that. It was very funny at times, but every single character is deeply cynical to their core. Maybe this captured a late 90's moment, apparently it did, but this isn't for me.

Snappy writing, memorable performances, and a blend of dark humor with raw emotion make this a striking portrait of suburban life. The character dynamics are tightly woven, with every plotline influencing the next, and the dialogue rarely wastes a word. Backed by Conrad Hall’s expressive visuals and Thomas Newman’s hauntingly simple score, it’s a film that feels both timeless and distinctly of its era, balancing satire and sincerity in a way that still resonates.

One of the best scripts and character development movies ive ever seen, the characters are all complex, fleshed out and interesting, the dialogue is believable, the human struggles are real and the bubbling under the surface resentment and frustration is there to see for each character and how they all interchange and mix in with eachothers story is so well done. The acting across the board is top notch, say whatever you want about Kevin Spacey but the man is one of the most natural actors ever. Can see why this is a classic, not 5/5 worthy but not far off either

A high-school project of a script and directing to match that is trying far too hard to labour a point that America thinks it is great but wait, no it isn't. Sorry, rest of the world already knew this in spades. Can't flaw the acting of Kevin Spacey, who could only work with what he was given. A bit of a yawn.

I hate all the characters in this film. There is an irony to the fact that movie talks about how "there's so much beauty in the world, I feel like I can't take it." considering every character finds the most negative outlook in every situation they're in. Now I need to relax and try to stop holding on to all this rage. The music and acting are both fine. I just hate the characters and story.

This really is an incredible movie. There are a couple of things a take question over after watching it agian, a decade or so later. But, think that’s natural with the subject matter. Its a pretty great movie with incredible character development and also destruction.

Revisiting American Beauty 25 years later gave me a different perspective of the 1999 classic. Alan Ball created a beautiful ending for his story, the kind of conclusion that makes "Citizen Kane" so memorable. The secret is to make the entire film imperative to the appreciation of the ending. The true brilliance of Ball's writing is in how each character's demons are melded together to create the climax. Spacey's Lester is the central focus and, like him or not, you are going to want to follow him on his journey.
Hating on Kevin Spacey is retconning one of the best performances of all time. This movie stands the test of time. I connected with it in 1999 and again in 2025.

“And then I remember to relax, and stop trying to hold on to it, and then it flows through me like rain, and I can't feel anything but gratitude for every single moment of my stupid little life.” Kevin spacey has always been an extremely talented actor. Watching him take on this role so well kind of bites a little to close to home. However, for brief moments throughout this movie, you will find yourself reliving the glory days when Kevin Spacey dominated cinema. You can lose yourself in this movie. That’s one of the sure signs that you are watching something great.
I thought it was gross as a teenager when I saw it in the theater. My opinion has only strengthened over time.

American Beauty shows the landscape of America in the 1990s. The isolation, the lost feeling,.. The scenes with spacey and Suvari are a little more creepy knowing Spacey and his record. But that is what the film wants to say. Overall a flawed but interesting look at America through the keyhole of a 'typical family'. Not pretty, but you can't turn away.

Ah yes- the most controversial “In hindsight” Best Picture of our entire lives. Up front: the main goals of the movie can be difficult to digest, even for a work of fiction. That said, it’s a tremendously well-made movie by Hollywood’s best actors, and one of the best working directors. True, the main idea especially can’t be endorsed. But to it’s credit the movie has guts to go difficult places, and it manages to do this well, with a fair mix humor amidst the heaviness throughout.

It doesn't surprise me that Kevin Spacey ended up being a creep in real life, he played his role too well lol. Overall, I was uncomfortable during the majority of the movie and it was d-i-s-t-u-r-b-i-n-g from beginning to end. Regardless, it was great.
The single best movie of all time. Even twenty five years later with multiple viewings, it’s as beautiful and moving as ever.
"American Beauty" is a great character, psychological study film. It has great balance. When there's comedy, we laugh, when there is tragedy, we sympathize for the "fallen hero."
"It's hard to stay mad when there's so much beauty in the world." American Beauty is a brilliant debut from director Sam Mendes.
It's a nice movie. But I can't believe Spacey got oscar for this, lol...
Learn this in english it sucks, especially when my teacher analyses Lesters strokes probably the best moment, like what language techniques are in that
Flawless. Timeless but also a glimpse into the late 90s.

Rewatched again for the first time in years & it's safe to say this multi layered & insightful look into dysfunctional suburban America still packs a punch, Spacey steals the show as irrelevant & downtrodden Lester Burnham, who goes through a midlife crisis after losing his job & fantasizing over his daughter's high school friend. Special mention to Chris Cooper as Colonel Frank Fitts who has his own demons nearly steals the show.

American Beauty is a dark comedy that follows the Burnham family as they deal with midlife crises, infidelity, and teenage rebellion. It’s a wild ride that explores themes of sexuality, materialism, and redemption.
Creepy creeperton. Kevin Spacey is a felon
An absolute masterpiece.
Simply brilliant .....a must watch, enjoy !
While it's difficult to quite understand what the makers set out to say, the movie is impactful and even scary in a unique way.
this movie is such a meme

A film often called overrated and outdated. In my opinion way off. This is still a very affective and incredible film. With of great acting, great cinematography, great music, and all under two hours. It’s still relevant! Yes 9/11 changed America afterward but people are still deeply unsatisfied with their perfect little so called American Dream lives. This film captures that feeling perfectly. And the garage scene has got to be one of the most underrated suspense sequences I’ve ever seen. In a decade of Best Picture history epics like Braveheart, Forrest Gump, and Titanic to have one set in quiet little suburbia say so much about characters with such stupid little lives speaks volumes of its greatness.
An absolute masterpiece and my favourite movie. That plastic bag has got that beauty, and the rest of the movie too! This movie could not be improved.
Excellent. Although a little creepy.

American Beauty never fully develops its too many characters and lacks in any sort of unique personality. It never became daring or provocative enough for the subject matter and doesn't live up to the full potential of its concept. This film lacks heavily in any amount of heart or sincerity, and it comes off less as a valid criticism of suburbia, and more of a mockery of it by someone who doesn't know anything about it. This film could've easily been saved by focusing more on the characters and their possible complexities than being a pretentious film that ultimately never knows what it's talking about.

Perfect in my eyes. It has everything in it. The cast were awesome. They acted the scenes wonderfully. My eyes were closed before watching this innocent of the world, then it got opened. Opened to the harsh reality of life.

In my top 30 all time favourite movies Dark, wickedly dark Kevin’s spacey and Annette Bening were triumphant, in this film that kinda reminds me of another film about a sparring couple, the war of the roses. Drama, wit, sex, and murder What else do you want? 10/10
A classic movie on a dysfunctional family.

American Beauty is on a shortlist of the greatest films ever made. An exceptionally well written screenplay brought to life by an exceptionally talented cast. We are privileged to see such a beautiful piece of art in our lifetime. Be grateful and watch this film. 100/100 🏆
